probe_1_iv_error.isolates: FNO vs SPICE drain current along transient trajectory (Vgs=0.85-0.90V)
probe_1_iv_error.note: Transient Vgs=0.85-0.90V; weak-inversion locus not traversed by step stimulus

probe_2_kcl_residual.isolates: KCL imbalance at pinned SPICE V_out vs pinned FNO V_out

probe_3_substitution.reason: Transient Vgs=0.85-0.90V throughout; narrow threshold (0.50V) never triggers. Broad threshold causes NR divergence: FNO Jacobian and SPICE current values are inconsistent when all timesteps route to cache. Probe 2 KCL ratio (268x) is the causal evidence.

transient_attribution.conclusion: NR is stable on this trajectory (alpha=1.0 every iteration, 3 iterations). The Stage-5 Jacobian diag ratio (~616) is a cheap max(|diag|)/min(|diag|) proxy, not cond(J); the healthy-solver claim rests on alpha and iteration count, not that ratio alone. 19.6uA KCL imbalance at SPICE V_out vs 73nA at FNO V_out (268x ratio). FNO converges correctly to wrong IV surface at nominal bias (Vgs~0.85V). Numerics do not contribute.

probe_1_iv_error.isolates: FNO vs SPICE drain current at each VTC bias point (fixed SPICE terminals)
probe_1_iv_error.pfet_spike_note: At Vin~0.25V (VSG~0.33V) FNO predicts ~13.7A vs SPICE 785pA - catastrophic near-off failure. PFET mean-capped ratio reflects 1000x clip; median and geomean are the robust alternatives.

substitution.isolates: brentq KCL solve on SPICE IV cache in bad region; FNO in good region
substitution.good_region_note: Good region (Vin >= threshold) is never routed to the SPICE cache by construction; collapse_fraction_good_mean ~ 0 confirms the gate is wired, not a separate empirical finding.

vtc_attribution.conclusion: Bad-region V_out error collapses by 97.5% on the mean (176.0 -> 4.35 mV) and 95.1% on the max (379.7 -> 18.72 mV) under brentq KCL substitution. Good-region error is unchanged by construction (gate routes only bad-region points to the cache). NFET shows a 4552x bad/good relative-Id ratio; PFET median bad-region relative-Id error is 14.2 vs good 0.053 (mean-capped ratio is a 1000x clip artefact). Weak-inversion IV error is causal for VTC failure with strong specificity.
